problem.title: 3Sum Closest
problem.description: Given an integer array nums of length n and an integer target, find three integers in nums such that the sum is closest to target.

example.explanation: The sum that is closest to the target is -1+2+1 = 2.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers
solutions.description: Sort the array and fix one element, then use two pointers to scan the remaining elements for the pair whose sum brings the triplet closest to the target. Moving the left pointer up when the sum is too small and the right pointer down when too large ensures every candidate pair is considered in O(n) per fixed element. The key insight is that sorting enables greedy pointer movement without missing the optimal answer.
